title: IF Staining for CD68
author: Eduard Bentea
materials: 250 µl blocking step : in 1 x PBS 0.1% Tergitol (ThermoScientific; 464252500; CAS: 68131-40-8) (PBS-T) + 10% Donkey serum (Jackson Immuno Research; 017-000-121; RRID: AB2337258) (accordingly to the secondary Abs) 00:40:00 incubation on the wobbler.
250 µl Primary Ab CD68 (Abcam; Ab 125212; RRID: AB_10975465) 1:500 in 1 x PBS-T + 10% Donkey serum (accordingly to the secondary Abs) Overnight at Room temperature on the wobbler.

steps.title: Add 500 µL 1 Molarity (M)...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL 1 Molarity (M) Citrate buffer ( pH 6 ) in each well. Make sure the tissue is flat and completely in contact with the buffer!

steps.title: 00:30:00 incubation at 80 °C ,...
steps.instruction: 00:30:00 incubation at 80 °C , 00:20:00 in the same buffer On ice .

steps.title: 250 µl secondary Ab 2 h...
steps.instruction: 250 µl secondary Ab 2 h 1/500 in 1 x PBS-T 02:00:00 Room temperature on the wobbler (dark by covering with aluminium foil).

steps.title: Briefly rinse sections in ½ PBS...
steps.instruction: Briefly rinse sections in ½ PBS + ½ AD , mount them on a microscope slide (Avantor VWR Microscope slides cut edges frosted; 631-1551) and allow them to dry Overnight while covered.

steps.title: Cover with 80 µL mowiol and...
steps.instruction: Cover with 80 µL mowiol and DAPI 1/500 (stains nucleus, 405 nm). Use cover slips #1.5 (Epredia; BB02400500SC13MNZ0). Let dry Overnight .

steps.title: Visualise the stained sections with Leica...
steps.instruction: Visualise the stained sections with Leica DM6 B LED Fluorescent microscope and the LasX software.
